info.name: Merchant Fleet
info.attribution:
Layout, design, coding, and library image by LawDawg96. Released to the Public Domain under CC0.

Caravel ship icon by Amethyst Studio available under CC BY 3.0 from https://thenounproject.com/icon/caravel-5296171/.

All other icons have attribution as listed in the About overlay.

Wood grain background by RyanPro available under the Pixabay license from https://pixabay.com/photos/trees-wood-yellow-wood-oak-1915247/
info.description: Select ships from a deck with a simplified auction and prepare your fleet to be the most successful traders by the end of three rounds.
info.helpText:
Players take seats. Press the Restart button to begin. 

Detailed usability information is found in the bottom middle of the screen by hovering over the information boxes.

fields.text: This will completely reset the board and start a new game. Are you sure?

clickRoutine: var playerCount = length ${activeSeats}
var removeCards = 6 - ${playerCount}
var removeCards = ${removeCards} * 3
var removeCards = 8 + ${removeCards}

fields.text: This will set up the board for the next round. Are you sure?

clickRoutine: var playerCount = length ${activeSeats}
var removeCards = 6 - ${playerCount}
var removeCards = ${removeCards} * 3
var removeCards = 8 + ${removeCards}

clickRoutine: // If you always want this seat to be a specific color, add a `staticColor` property wtih the hex color as the value

// If you want this seat to do other things, scroll all the way to the bottom and lock for comments about adding functions depending on whether the seat is being used or not. If you need functions that activate whenever there is a status change (being used or not), add those at the end of this clickRoutine.
// Check if another player is picking this seat

thenRoutine: // If another player is currently picking this seat, notify the play and end

fields.text: Pick another seat. Another player is getting this one ready.

elseRoutine: // If another player does not have the INPUT overlay on this seat active then flag it as active and CALL the routines needed to use the seat
// Update player color and name to match player's selections.

colorContrastRoutine: // Use color functions to automatically adjust contrast for best visibility
doubleClickRoutine: // A quick way to enter a seat with current playerName and random available color.
updateColorArrayRoutine: // Select all active seats except the current one and get color into an array.
// Remove colors used in other seats from the list of available colors for this player's overlay choices

thenRoutine: // If seat is empty, then prompt for name (and color if not static).

elseRoutine.header: Enter your name and choice of player color.

thenRoutine: // If seat is occupied by the player pressing the button, allow player to change name, color if not static, or get up from seat.

elseRoutine.header: Enter/change your name and choice of player color (or leave seat).

elseRoutine: // If seat is occupied by other than the player clicking, then confirm removal.

elseRoutine.header: Are you sure you want to remove ${PROPERTY player} from this seat?

fields.label: Remove this player from their seat

useSeatRoutine: // If player is leaving or getting kicked from seat, update color and player properties to empty values

elseRoutine: // Evaluate scenarios for what properties need to change

thenRoutine: // Double check to make sure that another player has not chosen the same color if multiple seat INPUT overlays were open at the same time
// Make updates to player color and name array for the return to the clickRoutine and SET the properties on this seat

fields.text: Choose again. That color was recently selected by another player.

thenRoutine: // If there is anything else this seat needs to do after a player has left, add that here.
elseRoutine: // If there is anything else this seat needs to do after it has been claimed, do that here.

fields.label: Type the number of points gained or lost. Put a - before the number to lose points.

fields.text: This will reveal your victory points to all players. Are you sure?

endgame.detail:
Game End Scoring
After scoring the 3rd round, add the value in the lower left for each of the 3 categories of the promotion tiles ( ) for each player. Rank each player for each category. Give victory points as follows:

1st Place: 30
2nd Place: 20
3rd Place: 10

The rules do not specify how to break ties to determine player rank, but using the position of the player with the higher marker on the orange scroll track () is one commonly accepted method.

At this point, scoring is completed. Click the "Show VP" button (visibility) to reveal your victory points to other players. The highest number of victory points wins. In case of a tie, the player with the higher position on the orange scroll track () wins.

gameplay.detail:
Setup
Once players are seated, press the "Restart" button (restart_alt). A random player is selected to start and the game is set up for the first ship auction.

Press the "Turn" button (skip_next) to change the turn indicator on the seat.

After each round once victory points have been calculated and entered, press the "Round" button in the lower right (but it is actually square-shaped; ironic isn't it?) to set up the board for the next round. Information about how to use the other button automation in the room is in the other hoverable player aids.
Card Distribution
Ship cards: 29 total, but some are out of play depending on player count. There are four each with values 1-6, three 7s, and two 8s.

Promotion tiles: There are three with value 1 and (), three with value 1 and (), three with value 1 and (), and three with value 2 and no commodity.

actions.detail:
Turn Sequence
The player whose turn it is can place a claim marker (in a holder on the top right of the card) on the ship up for auction or can pass. The commodity marker ( ) and the (+1) marker can only be used if another player has not yet claimed the ship. Only the pirate claim marker () can beat a marker already placed by another player. Claim marker features:

+1: Increases the value of the ship by 1 (for example, a 5 ship becomes a 6).
Commodity: The ship is worth 1 additional commodity of the type at the track where it is placed.
Pirate: The player takes the ship immediately. If a marker was already on the ship, return it to the other player.

Once a ship is claimed, play it on one of the the 3 commodity scoring tracks ( ). When placing a ship, comply with the following:
  • Each player can only put 1 ship on each track during a round.
  • Drop ships into the holder next to the scoring track and place in descending order. Ships won with a (+1) claim marker are treated as 1 higher than the value on the card. If there is a tie, the new ship is placed below the existing ship.
  • Move the marker up the scoring track 1 space for each corresponding symbol on the ship card plus 1 additional space if the ship has the commodity claim marker. If more than one player is on the same track position, the player that got there first is on the left.
  • If the ship card has 1 or 2 orange scrolls (), move that player's marker up the orange scroll track that many spaces.
  • If the ship card has a promotion tile (), take one of the 3 face-up promotion tiles next to the orange track. Move the tile to the holder next to that player's seat. If the tile has a commodity on it, advance that player's marker 1 space up the corresponding track. Manually replace the used tile by dragging one from the holder above the face up tiles so there are always 3 to choose from.
The next player in turn order (top to bottom in this implementation) draws the next ship card by pressing the "Flip" button (). Players only get one chance to place a marker on each card. If all players pass, discard it by dragging it to the discard holder (). That player goes first on the next ship.

Play continues until all players have used all of their claim markers or the ship pile is empty. There are only 3 extra ships for each round. Any player that has already used all of their claim markers does not participate in more auctions that round.

domains.detail:
Round End Scoring
Score victory points for the highest number ship on each of the 3 commodity scoring tracks ( ) and excluding the orange scroll track (). A ship with a +1 claim is valued at 1 higher than the value on the card. A tie goes to ship placed first:

1st Place: 15
2nd Place: 10
3rd Place: 5

Score victory points for the highest marker on all 4 scoring tracks. A tie goes to the marker on the left that got there first:

1st Place: 15
2nd Place: 10
3rd Place: 5

Additionally, any player with a marker on the top 3 bonus spaces score additional victory points of 5, 10, or 15 points depending on location.

To add victory points to your score, click the area near that seat with a number and "vp" and enter a value. Victory points remain hidden to other players until after round 3 at the end of the game.

The player that has advanced highest on the orange scroll track () goes first in the next round.

objects.css: background-color:#deb887;border: 2px solid black; border-radius:8px; text-align: center
